name: KOS-1584
description: KOS-1584 is a second-generation epothilone. Epothilones are anticancer agents with a taxane-like mechanism of action that have demonstrated activity in taxane-resistant tumors. KOS-1584 is a second-generation compound with increased potency, favorable tissue distribution, and ease of formulation.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in solid tumors.
mechanismOfAction: Epothilones are highly potent microtubulin stabilizing compounds with a similar mechanism of action to taxanes and broad applicability in a wide range of tumors. Epothilones are active in both taxane-sensitive and taxane-resistant cancers and have demonstrated low susceptibility to tumor resistance mechanisms.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of KOS-1584.

interactions.name: Paclitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with KOS-1584.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Docetaxel is combined with KOS-1584.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Cabazitaxel is combined with KOS-1584.
